key_themes: Iran war entering third week — 6,000+ targets struck, 1,348 civilians killed, new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vows continued resistance
Strait of Hormuz remains mined and effectively closed — IEA approves historic 400M barrel SPR release
Oil above $100/bbl — US gas at $3.63/gal (22-month high) — inflation pass-through expected March/April
Israel expands Lebanon operations dramatically — 680+ killed, 800,000 displaced, IDF past Litani River
USTR launches dual Section 301 investigations: overcapacity (16 economies, 21 sectors) + forced labor (60 countries)
North Korea fires 10 ballistic missiles during US-Korea Freedom Shield exercise (18,000 troops)
France announces unprecedented 10-warship deployment to Hormuz region
Russia-Ukraine peace talks stalled at '90% complete' — Russia benefits from oil windfall
Belgium paralyzed by 24-hour general strike — all flights cancelled
Trump launches Shield of Americas alliance with 15 Latin American nations; recognizes Venezuela's Rodriguez

top_risk_deteriorations.reason: War week 3 — 6,000+ targets struck — Mojtaba Khamenei installed — economy in freefall
Lebanon war expansion — 680+ killed — multi-front conflict deepening
No pipeline alternative to Hormuz — near-total economic paralysis continues
World's largest LNG exporter offline — jets in direct combat with Iranian bombers
War week 3 + Section 301 escalation + 217-214 House margin + bond stress
DPRK 10-missile salvo + Section 301 + US military stretched

top_beneficiaries.reason: Oil windfall + US distracted + peace talks stalled in Russia's favor
North Sea producer — non-Hormuz — European gas demand redirecting
Net oil exporter — geographically insulated — commodity basket elevated
Asserting defense leadership — 10-warship deployment to Hormuz
Atlantic coast oil exports secure — $100+ oil windfall
Net energy exporter via Vaca Muerta — Milei reform credibility rising

summary.ai_disclosure: All trend estimates are AI-generated based on publicly available information as of 2026-03-14. These are probabilistic estimates subject to uncertainty and should be validated against primary sources. Confidence scores reflect estimated reliability.

estimates.reasoning: US GDP stable but facing cross-currents. Energy self-sufficiency provides buffer, but Iran war costs, Section 301 trade escalation against 76 trading partners, and oil shock consumer impact create headwinds. February CPI benign but forward-looking indicators deteriorating. 5 recession warning signs flagged.
supporting_evidence: War spending adding to fiscal pressure
estimates.investor_implication: US GDP stable but recession risk rising from war costs and trade escalation.

estimates.reasoning: US February CPI steady at 2.4% but pre-dates Iran war oil shock. Gas prices at $3.63/gal (22-month high). March/April readings will capture $100+ oil pass-through. IEA 400M barrel SPR release provides partial but insufficient offset.
supporting_evidence: IEA 400M barrel SPR release — largest ever
counter_arguments: SPR release may cap oil prices temporarily
estimates.investor_implication: US inflation re-accelerating from energy shock. Fed rate cut path delayed.

estimates.reasoning: US current account roughly stable. Energy self-sufficiency buffers oil shock. Section 301 investigations (16 overcapacity + 60 forced labor) could reduce imports medium-term. War costs add to transfers. Safe-haven dollar inflows support.
counter_arguments: War costs adding to external transfers
estimates.investor_implication: US current account stable. Competing forces offsetting.

estimates.reasoning: US debt rising from war spending (Operation Epic Fury) entering third week. Senate failed to pass war powers resolution — no legislative check on spending. $175B tariff refund SCOTUS liability. GOP House majority razor-thin at 217-214 after Kiley exit. Defense supplementals inevitable.
supporting_evidence: War spending unchecked after failed war powers vote
counter_arguments: Dollar reserve status provides financing advantage
estimates.investor_implication: US debt trajectory accelerating. War costs plus political constraints on fiscal policy.

estimates.reasoning: USD strengthening as safe-haven during Middle East war. DXY at multi-month highs. War and global risk-off supporting dollar demand.
counter_arguments: War costs and fiscal expansion long-term negative

estimates.reasoning: US 10yr yields rising on war spending, inflation expectations from oil shock, and fiscal uncertainty from $175B tariff refund liability. Bond market stress from leveraged basis trade ($1T+) echoes March 2020.
supporting_evidence: Bond market stress from basis trade unwind
counter_arguments: Fed could intervene if market dysfunctional
estimates.investor_implication: US yields rising. Bond market stress elevated.

estimates.reasoning: Fed on hold at 3.625%. February CPI steady at 2.4% pre-war but March/April readings will capture oil shock. Central bank super week March 16-19 — stagflation dilemma limits rate cuts. War uncertainty keeps Fed cautious.
estimates.investor_implication: Fed on hold. Rate cut path delayed by energy inflation.

estimates.reasoning: US national power stable but stretched across multiple theaters. Iran war entering third week. Freedom Shield exercise in Korea while DPRK fires 10 missiles. Shield of Americas alliance launched with 15 Latin American nations. Section 301 investigations demonstrate trade leverage.

estimates.investor_implication: US power projection tested but alliance-building continues.

estimates.reasoning: US investment risk rising from war costs, bond market stress, Section 301 trade escalation, and political fragility (217-214 House majority). Senate failed to constrain war spending. 5 recession warning signs. But institutional strength provides floor.
estimates.investor_implication: US risk rising but from strong base. War duration key variable.

estimates.reasoning: US defense spending growing from Iran war (Operation Epic Fury) now entering third week. Freedom Shield exercise in Korea with 18,000 troops. Shield of Americas alliance with 15 Latin American nations. SIPRI: global arms transfers +9.2%, US exports +27%.
estimates.investor_implication: US defense spending accelerating. Defense stocks benefiting.

estimates.reasoning: US political stability declining. War with Iran has no congressional authorization — war powers vote failed. supporting_evidence: War powers vote failed — no congressional authorization
